violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Cut melon was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit on the breakfast cold line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cut melon was placed in the refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The consumer advisory provided does accurately disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked, and includes eggs prepared YOUR WAY on the Kid's Plate child menu. Menu is provided by Hilton. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Employee observed entering the kitchen and starting to handle equipment without washing their hands. When To Wash poster given to PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Two containers of moldy strawberries observed in the refrigerator. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. 3 bay sink will be used for sanitizing until dishwasher is adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

Yogurt and cut melon observed held at 46-48 degrees Fahrenheit at cold line. Turkey, ham, cut tomatoes, and chicken salad observed at 58-60 degrees on the counter, awaiting preparation while staff completed other tasks. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed into the refrigerator to chill. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Foods observed date marked with a range allowing over 7 days. Reviewed proper date marking with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

The consumer advisory provided does accurately disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked, and includes eggs prepared YOUR WAY on the Kid's Plate child menu. Menu is provided by Hilton. New Violation. Correct By: 04/05/2026
Ice bucket stored not stored inverted. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Inspector observed 2 oranges in the walk-in with a white substance on the skin. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2025

Wiping cloths and spray bottles were observed hanging on the bar hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

Several food items were observed held at 47-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the cold drawers. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods requiring refrigeration were disposed, all other items moved to other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025
ROP salmon was observed thawing with packaging intact. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025
The cold drawers on the grill line were observed in poor repair, holding an ambient temperature of 58 degrees. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -table-top under grill -bottom shelf of low boy freezer -walk-in fan guards. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025
The air curtains of the walk-in are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. PIC stated they are enrolled in course to reinstate certification and will register with RIDOH once completed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
A pitcher was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
The hand washing sink on the grill line is lacking proper signage. All other sinks were observed with proper signage during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was on site at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Dishwasher located in the bar area was not in good repair. Service was on-hand at the time of inspection to repair leak. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen grill area and the bar were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

A pitcher was observed in the hand-washing sink in the grill area. A spray bottle and clean rags were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen are lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Food contact surfaces of the following equipment/utensils were observed with an accumulation of food and debris: -ice machine (taken out of service, ice disposed) -utensil container at the bar -can opener -knife. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwashers were 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. All equipment and utensils to be sanitized in 3-bay sink until resolved. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Staff must manually fill 3-bay sink to proper concentration until automatic dispenser is adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Sausage was observed held at 132 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated to 165 degrees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Walk-in cooler was observed holding at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods requiring refrigeration moved to other unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Deli meats, cheeses, cut vegetables were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
Liquid egg replacer was observed in walk-in with an expiration date of June 17, 2024. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

ROP salmon observed thawing in the walk-in with packaging intact. As per package label, product must be removed from package prior to thawing. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Working containers of multiple dry ingredients and sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of sauces. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC removed bowl and obtained a ladle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
Cleaned and sanitized carafes are stored on the floor at the bar area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
Dishwasher located in the bar area was not in good repair, observed leaking during operation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The dishwashers were observed not dispensing sanitizer as evidenced by test strip reading 0 parts per million. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machines. Inspector provided a small supply of test strips during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-spice shelf-under prep unit hood-under grill-reach-in gaskets-bottom of freezer-bottom of hot box-robot coupe base-top of dish machine (kitchen) New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the dumpster area. Inspector observed broken/unused equipment and paint cans in the dumpster enclosure. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The floors throughout the kitchen, particularly at the floor/wall junctures and behind/under equipment have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
